diagrams.title: Pointer Variable and Memory Address
diagrams.description: A diagram showing a regular variable storing a value and a pointer variable storing the memory address of that variable, with an arrow visually connecting them to demonstrate the pointer 'pointing' to the variable.
elements: Memory block representing an integer variable 'x' with value 42
Memory block representing a pointer variable 'ptr' with address 0x1000
Arrow from pointer to the variable it points to
Label showing the address-of operator (&) and dereference operator (*)
Color-coded boxes: blue for regular variable, green for pointer
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental concept that a pointer stores a memory address rather than a value, and shows how the pointer references another variable through its address.

diagrams.title: Reference Variable as Alias
diagrams.description: A diagram showing a reference variable as an alias for another variable, depicting both names pointing to the SAME memory location, demonstrating that they are not separate storage but two names for one location.
elements: Single memory block representing variable 'x' with value 100
Two labels attached to the same memory block: 'x' and 'ref'
Visual indication that both names refer to the identical memory location
Dotted box around the single memory location containing both names
Arrow showing they both access the same data
diagrams.purpose: Clarifies that a reference is NOT a new variable or a copy, but simply an alternative name (alias) for an existing variable, sharing the same memory location.

diagrams.title: Pointer vs Reference Comparison
diagrams.description: A side-by-side comparison diagram showing the differences between pointers and references: pointer can be null, must use dereference operator (*), can be reassigned; reference must be initialized, cannot be null, cannot be reassigned.
elements: Left side: Pointer diagram showing pointer variable with address, arrow to target, and the dereferenced value accessed via *ptr
Right side: Reference diagram showing reference as alias to variable
Comparison table below showing: Null capability, Reassignment ability, Declaration syntax (int* vs int&), Dereference requirement
Visual icons: pointer with asterisk *, reference with ampersand &
Color coding to differentiate pointer (green) from reference (orange)
diagrams.purpose: Provides a clear visual comparison of pointers and references, highlighting their differences in syntax, behavior, and capabilities to help learners understand when to use each.
